GroupResponseGroupData.description: Information about objects matching the given key.

aggregation_count.description:
Number of objects in group.
This field is always set.

aggregation_uuids.description:
List of UUIDs of the objects in the group.
Only populated if show_aggregation_uuids is set.

aggregation_value.description: Value of the aggregation operation as calculated by the query.

unique_counts.description:
Map of counts for the given unique_count_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_count_paths is set.

unique_values.description:
Map of values for the given unique_value_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_value_paths is set.

JIRAConfigJiraIssueType.description:
Deprecated: This enum will not be used. A string field will be used instead.

 - JIRA_ISSUE_TYPE_TASK: Indicates a Jira task.
 - JIRA_ISSUE_TYPE_BUG: Indicates a Jira Bug.

NotificationTargetActionActionType.description:
- ACTION_TYPE_WEBHOOK: ACTION_TYPE_WEBHOOK indicates a webhook call action.
 - ACTION_TYPE_JIRA: ACTION_TYPE_JIRA indicates a JIRA action.
 - ACTION_TYPE_EMAIL: ACTION_TYPE_EMAIL indicates an email action.
 - ACTION_TYPE_VANTA: ACTION_TYPE_VANTA indicates configuration of a Vanta plugin.
 - ACTION_TYPE_SLACK: ACTION_TYPE_SLACK indicates a Slack integration.
 - ACTION_TYPE_GITHUB_PR: ACTION_TYPE_GITHUB_PR indicates a GitHub PR action.

action_type.description:
Indicates an action that needs to be taken in response to a policy violation.
For example, JIRA, Webhook, or an email notification.

exclude_endor_url.description: Excludes the URL to access the Endor Labs app which provides the notification details.

public.description:
Indicates if the action endpoint is public.
If the endpoint not public, then endorctl attempts to create the action as if the endpoint is running on-prem.

NotificationTargetServiceCreateNotificat.description: Indicates actions taken when a corresponding notification is raised.

propagate.description: Indicates whether the object should be visible in the child namespaces or not.

tenant_meta.description: Notification Targets are associated with a tenant.
tenant_meta.title: Notification Targets are associated with a tenant.

uuid.description: The UUID of the notification target.

object.description: Indicates actions taken when a corresponding notification is raised.

VantaConfigVantaAuthMethod.description:
- VANTA_AUTH_METHOD_ACCESS_TOKEN: Uses basic username and password and retrieves an ephemeral access token for authentication.
 - VANTA_AUTH_METHOD_ACCESS_API_KEY: Uses an API key provided by the user.

Note: This is just a placeholder and not yet implemented.

WebhookConfigWebhookAuthMethod.description:
- WEBHOOK_AUTH_METHOD_NONE: Uses no authentication. Not recommended.
 - WEBHOOK_AUTH_METHOD_BASIC: Uses basic username and password.
 - WEBHOOK_AUTH_METHOD_API_KEY: Uses an API key as credentials.

WebhookConfigWebhookHeader.description:
A single HTTP header attached to every webhook request, regardless of
the configured auth_method. Use this for vendor-specific auth headers
(e.g., DD-API-KEY for Datadog) or arbitrary metadata headers.

name.description:
Header name. Must be a valid RFC 7230 token (letters, digits, and
!#$%&'*+-.^_`|~). Reserved names (Content-Type, Authorization when an
auth_method other than NONE is set, X-Endor-HMAC-Signature, and
hop-by-hop headers) are rejected at send time.

value.description:
Header value sent verbatim. Stored as a secret (encrypted at rest,
redacted in API responses) because values often hold API keys.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

v1CountResponse.description: Response to a list count request.

count.description: Number of objects matching the given list parameters.

v1CustomTemplate.description: CustomTemplate is the type specific custom template.

open_action_template.description: The template to be used for OPEN action for email plugin.

resolve_action_template.description: The template to be used for RESOLVE action for email plugin.

update_action_template.description: The template to be used for UPDATE action for email plugin.

v1GroupAggregationValueResponse.description:
GroupAggregationValueResponse returns the value of the aggregation if
requested.

v1GroupResponse.description: Response to a list group request.

v1IndexData.description:
IndexData is used to index the resource for search. It's an internal
object.

search_score.description: search_score is the score of the resource for search. Internal use only.

will_be_deleted_at.description: Time that the resource will be deleted.

api_key.description: The API Key to authenticate with Jira.

bearer_token.description: The bearer token to authenticate with Jira.

cloud_id.description: The Jira Cloud ID required to route API requests through the Atlassian API gateway.

components.description: The Components field in Jira board.

issue_type.description: The issue Type to raise in Jira.

labels.description: The labels to apply to the Jira issue.

project_key.description: The project key in which the Jira issue is raised as an action for the notification.

resolve_state.description: The final ticket status after resolving.

use_bearer_token.description: Denotes if we should use bearer token to authenticate with Jira.

user_name.description: The user name to authenticate with Jira.

v1ListNotificationTargetsResponse.description: Response returned for requests to list notification targets.

next_page_id.description: The UUID of last message retrieved.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token that can be used to retrieve the next page of results.

v1Meta.description: Common fields for all Endor Labs resources.

annotations.description:
Annotations can be used to attach metadata to a resource message.
Annotation values can be small or large, structured or unstructured,
and may include characters not permitted by labels.
The keys may contain alphanumerics, underscores (_), dots (.) and dashes
(-). The values of an annotation must be 16384 bytes or smaller.

create_time.description:
Time the resource was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

created_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the user who created the object,
for example, ewok@endor.ai@google@api-key.

description.description: Resource description. Must be less than 1024 bytes.

kind.description:
Resource kind, for example, HelloResponse.
Auto-generated using the protobuf message proto.MessageName().Name().

name.description: Resource name. Must be 63 characters or less.

parent_kind.description: Parent object resource kind, for example, Project.

references.description: Map of objects referenced in a query API.

tags.description:
List of tags attached to the resource.
Tags can be used to select objects and to find collections of objects that
satisfy certain conditions. A tag must be 255 characters or less.

update_time.description:
Time the resource was last updated.
Note: Updated on all create/patch/delete operations.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

updated_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the last user who updated the object,
for example, vulnerabilityingestor@endor.ai@x509.

upsert_time.description:
Time the resource was last upserted.

Note:
create_time is only set the first time the resource is created.
upsert_time is set every time the resource is upseted.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

propagate.description: Indicates whether the object should be visible in the child namespaces or not.

uuid.description: The UUID of the notification target.

custom_template.description:
The custom template to be used for the notification.
If not available, the default template is used.

findings_summary_template.description: The template for the PR comment for summary of findings.

open_action_template.description: The template to be used for OPEN action for slack plugin.

update_action_template.description: The template to be used for UPDATE action for slack plugin.

v1TenantMeta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

namespace.description:
Namespaces are a way to organize organizational units into virtual
groupings of resources. Namespaces must be a fully qualified name,
for example, the child namespace of namespace "endor.prod" called "app"
is called "endor.prod.app".

v1UpdateRequest.description: Message used for all update requests.

force.description:
Force will force the update of the resource if any
checks fail.

update_mask.description: Fields to update. Defaults to all fields.

package_vulnerability_resource_id.description: The Resource ID of the package vulnerability created in Vanta.

sca_resource_id.description: The Resource ID of the static code analysis vulnerabilities created in Vanta.

vulnerable_component_resource_id.description: The Resource ID of the vulnerable component created in Vanta.

custom_headers.description:
Additional HTTP headers attached to every webhook request, evaluated
independently of auth_method. Enables forwarding to systems that
require non-Bearer auth headers (Datadog, Splunk, New Relic, Elastic).

open_action_template.description: The template to be used for OPEN action for webhook plugin.

resolve_action_template.description: The template to be used for RESOLVE action for webhook plugin.

update_action_template.description: The template to be used for UPDATE action for webhook plugin.
